description_long: This doll has a humanoid form with a skeletal, mechanical structure, featuring exposed wires and metal components throughout its body. Its head is round with large, hollow eye sockets and a gaping mouth, giving it a haunting, animatronic appearance. The limbs are thin and jointed, with claw-like hands and feet, and the entire figure is covered in twisted, tangled cables and fragments of decayed material. Two prominent, bent antennae or ear-like appendages protrude from the top of the head, adding to its unsettling, robotic look. The surface is a mix of dark, rusted metal, brownish decayed patches, and occasional hints of yellow and red, suggesting wear and corrosion. The overall impression is of a damaged, abandoned animatronic doll, possibly inspired by horror or urban decay themes.
description: A humanoid, animatronic doll with exposed wires, rusted metal, and claw-like hands and feet, featuring a haunting, skeletal appearance and bent antennae on its head.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the doll's hollow eyes, open mouth, and symmetrical, outstretched arms with clawed hands.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin, jointed limbs, protruding chest, and the depth of the tangled wires along the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the head, bent antennae, and the dense network of cables and exposed mechanical parts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the skeletal profile, visible wiring, and the curvature of the limbs.
attribute_clothing_style: No traditional clothing; the doll is covered in decayed, patchy fabric remnants and exposed mechanical parts, with no discernible fashion style.
attribute_pose: Standing upright with arms slightly bent and extended outward, legs straight and spaced apart, head facing forward, and antennae protruding upward.
attribute_facial_expression: Neutral, with hollow, wide-open eyes and a gaping mouth, giving a vacant, expressionless look.
attribute_hair_style: No visible hair; instead, two bent, wire-like antennae extend from the top of the head.
